faqs.question: What is backflow and why is it dangerous?
faqs.answer: Backflow occurs when contaminated water flows backward into the clean water supply. This can introduce chemicals, bacteria, and other hazardous substances into your drinking water, posing serious health risks.

faqs.question: How often does backflow testing need to be done?
faqs.answer: Most municipalities require annual backflow testing and certification. We handle the testing, paperwork, and filing with your local water authority.

faqs.question: What types of backflow prevention devices do you install?
faqs.answer: We install and service all types of backflow prevention devices including reduced pressure zone (RPZ) assemblies, double check valves, and pressure vacuum breakers.

faqs.question: What happens if my backflow preventer fails inspection?
faqs.answer: If your device fails, we can repair or replace it on the spot and retest to ensure compliance. We carry common parts and devices so most issues are resolved in a single visit. Contact us to schedule.
